<content>1: """
2: Comprehensive tests for hybrid search functionality.
3: 
4: Tests for dense_search(), sparse_search(), and hybrid_search() functions
5: with mocked database queries and embeddings.
6: """
7: 
8: from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch
9: 
10: import pytest
11: import torch
12: 
13: from khoj.database.models import Agent, Entry, KhojUser
14: from khoj.search_type import text_search
15: 
16: 
17: class MockQuerySet:
18:     """Mock Django QuerySet for testing."""
19: 
20:     def __init__(self, results):
21:         self._results = results
22: 
23:     def annotate(self, **kwargs):
24:         return self
25: 
26:     def filter(self, *args, **kwargs):
27:         # Handle agent and file_type filtering
28:         if "agent" in kwargs:
29:             agent = kwargs["agent"]
30:             filtered = [r for r in self._results if getattr(r, "agent", None) == agent]
31:             return MockQuerySet(filtered)
32:         if "file_type" in kwargs:
33:             file_type = kwargs["file_type"]
34:             filtered = [r for r in self._results if getattr(r, "file_type", None) == file_type]
35:             return MockQuerySet(filtered)
36:         return self
37: 
38:     def order_by(self, *args):
39:         # Simple mock - just return self
40:         return self
41: 
42:     def __getitem__(self, key):
43:         return MockQuerySet(self._results[key])
44: 
45:     def __iter__(self):
46:         return iter(self._results)
47: 
48:     def __len__(self):
49:         return len(self._results)
50: 
51:     def all(self):
52:         return self
53: 
54: 
55: def create_mock_entry(entry_id: int, content: str = None, **kwargs) -> MagicMock:
56:     """Create a mock Entry object for testing."""
57:     entry = MagicMock(spec=Entry)
58:     entry.id = entry_id
59:     entry.raw = content or f"Test content {entry_id}"
60:     entry.compiled = content or f"Test compiled {entry_id}"
61:     entry.heading = kwargs.get("heading", f"Test heading {entry_id}")
62:     entry.file_type = kwargs.get("file_type", Entry.EntryType.PLAINTEXT)
63:     entry.file_source = kwargs.get("file_source", Entry.EntrySource.COMPUTER)
64:     entry.file_path = kwargs.get("file_path", f"/test/path/{entry_id}.txt")
65:     entry.hashed_value = kwargs.get("hashed_value", f"hash_{entry_id}")
66:     entry.corpus_id = kwargs.get("corpus_id", f"corpus_{entry_id}")
67:     entry.url = kwargs.get("url", None)
68:     entry.agent = kwargs.get("agent", None)
69:     entry.user = kwargs.get("user", None)
70:     entry.distance = kwargs.get("distance", 0.5)
71:     return entry
72: 
73: 
74: class TestDenseSearch:
75:     """Tests for dense_search() function."""
76: 
77:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
78:     async def test_dense_search_basic(self, default_user):
79:         """Test basic dense search functionality."""
80:         # Arrange
81:         mock_entries = [
82:             create_mock_entry(1, "First test entry", distance=0.3),
83:             create_mock_entry(2, "Second test entry", distance=0.5),
84:         ]
85:         query_embedding = torch.tensor([0.1, 0.2, 0.3])
86: 
87:         with patch("khoj.search_type.text_search.EntryAdapters") as mock_adapter:
88:             mock_queryset = MockQuerySet(mock_entries)
89:             mock_adapter.search_with_embeddings.return_value = mock_queryset
90: 
91:             # Act
92:             results = await text_search.dense_search(
93:                 query_embedding=query_embedding,
94:                 user=default_user,
95:                 k=10,
96:             )
97: 
98:             # Assert
99:             assert len(results) == 2
100:             assert results[0].id == 1
101:             assert results[1].id == 2
102:             mock_adapter.search_with_embeddings.assert_called_once()
103: 
104:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
105:     async def test_dense_search_with_max_distance(self, default_user):
106:         """Test dense search with max_distance filter."""
107:         # Arrange
108:         query_embedding = torch.tensor([0.1, 0.2, 0.3])
109:         max_distance = 0.4
110: 
111:         with patch("khoj.search_type.text_search.EntryAdapters") as mock_adapter:
112:             mock_entries = [create_mock_entry(1, "Close match", distance=0.3)]
113:             mock_adapter.search_with_embeddings.return_value = MockQuerySet(mock_entries)
114: 
115:             # Act
116:             results = await text_search.dense_search(
117:                 query_embedding=query_embedding,
118:                 user=default_user,
119:                 k=10,
120:                 max_distance=max_distance,
121:             )
122: 
123:             # Assert
124:             mock_adapter.search_with_embeddings.assert_called_once()
125:             call_kwargs = mock_adapter.search_with_embeddings.call_args[1]
126:             assert call_kwargs["max_distance"] == max_distance
127: 
128:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
129:     async def test_dense_search_with_file_type_filter(self, default_user):
130:         """Test dense search with file_type filter."""
131:         # Arrange
132:         query_embedding = torch.tensor([0.1, 0.2, 0.3])
133:         file_type = Entry.EntryType.MARKDOWN
134: 
135:         with patch("khoj.search_type.text_search.EntryAdapters") as mock_adapter:
136:             mock_entries = [create_mock_entry(1, "Markdown entry", file_type=file_type)]
137:             mock_adapter.search_with_embeddings.return_value = MockQuerySet(mock_entries)
138: 
139:             # Act
140:             results = await text_search.dense_search(
141:                 query_embedding=query_embedding,
142:                 user=default_user,
143:                 k=10,
144:                 file_type=file_type,
145:             )
146: 
147:             # Assert
148:             mock_adapter.search_with_embeddings.assert_called_once()
149:             call_kwargs = mock_adapter.search_with_embeddings.call_args[1]
150:             assert call_kwargs["file_type_filter"] == file_type
151: 
152:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
153:     async def test_dense_search_with_agent_filter(self, default_user):
154:         """Test dense search with agent filter."""
155:         # Arrange
156:         query_embedding = torch.tensor([0.1, 0.2, 0.3])

269: class TestSparseSearch:
270:     """Tests for sparse_search() function."""
271: 
272:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
273:     async def test_sparse_search_basic(self, default_user):
274:         """Test basic sparse search functionality."""
275:         # Arrange
276:         query_text = "test query"
277:         mock_entries = [
278:             create_mock_entry(1, "First test entry"),
279:             create_mock_entry(2, "Second test entry"),
280:         ]
281: 
282:         with patch.object(Entry.objects, "annotate") as mock_annotate, \
283:              patch.object(Entry.objects, "filter") as mock_filter:
284:             mock_queryset = MagicMock()
285:             mock_queryset.order_by.return_value.__getitem__ = lambda self, key: MockQuerySet(mock_entries[:key.stop if hasattr(key, 'stop') else len(mock_entries)])
286:             mock_filter.return_value = mock_queryset
287:             mock_annotate.return_value.filter.return_value = mock_filter.return_value
288: 
289:             # We need to patch the entire queryset chain
290:             mock_chain = MagicMock()
291:             mock_chain.annotate.return_value = mock_chain
292:             mock_chain.filter.return_value = mock_chain
293:             mock_chain.order_by.return_value.__getitem__ = lambda k: MockQuerySet(mock_entries[:10])
294: 
295:             with patch.object(Entry.objects, "annotate", return_value=mock_chain):
296:                 # Act
297:                 results = await text_search.sparse_search(
298:                     query_text=query_text,
299:                     user=default_user,
300:                     k=10,
301:                 )
302: 
303:                 # Assert
304:                 assert len(results) == 2
305: 
306:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
307:     async def test_sparse_search_with_file_type_filter(self, default_user):
308:         """Test sparse search with file_type filter."""
309:         # Arrange
310:         query_text = "test query"
311:         file_type = Entry.EntryType.MARKDOWN
312: 
313:         with patch.object(Entry.objects, "annotate") as mock_annotate:
314:             mock_chain = MagicMock()
315:             mock_chain.annotate.return_value = mock_chain
316:             mock_chain.filter.return_value = mock_chain
317:             mock_chain.order_by.return_value.__getitem__ = lambda k: MockQuerySet([])
318: 
319:             mock_annotate.return_value = mock_chain
320: 
321:             # Act
322:             results = await text_search.sparse_search(
323:                 query_text=query_text,
324:                 user=default_user,
325:                 k=10,
326:                 file_type=file_type,
327:             )
328: 
329:             # Assert - verify chain was called with file_type filter
330:             mock_chain.filter.assert_any_call(file_type=file_type)
331:

409: class TestHybridSearch:
410:     """Tests for hybrid_search() function combining dense and sparse search."""
411: 
412:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
413:     @pytest.mark.parametrize("alpha", [0.3, 0.5, 0.7])
414:     async def test_hybrid_search_with_different_alpha_values(self, alpha, default_user):
415:         """Test hybrid search with different alpha weighting values."""
416:         # Arrange
417:         query_text = "test query"
418:         query_embedding = torch.tensor([0.1, 0.2, 0.3])
419: 
420:         # Create mock entries that appear in both dense and sparse results
421:         mock_entry_1 = create_mock_entry(1, "Common entry 1")
422:         mock_entry_2 = create_mock_entry(2, "Common entry 2")
423:         mock_entry_3 = create_mock_entry(3, "Dense only entry")
424:         mock_entry_4 = create_mock_entry(4, "Sparse only entry")
425: 
426:         dense_entries = [mock_entry_1, mock_entry_2, mock_entry_3]
427:         sparse_entries = [mock_entry_1, mock_entry_2, mock_entry_4]
428: 
429:         with patch("khoj.search_type.text_search.dense_search") as mock_dense, \
430:              patch("khoj.search_type.text_search.sparse_search") as mock_sparse:
431:             mock_dense.return_value = dense_entries
432:             mock_sparse.return_value = sparse_entries
433: 
434:             # Act
435:             results = await text_search.hybrid_search(
436:                 query_text=query_text,
437:                 query_embedding=query_embedding,
438:                 user=default_user,
439:                 k=10,
440:                 alpha=alpha,
441:             )
442: 
443:             # Assert
444:             assert len(results) <= 10
445:             # Verify both search methods were called
446:             mock_dense.assert_called_once()
447:             mock_sparse.assert_called_once()
448: 
449:             # Verify alpha was passed through (stored for future use)
450:             # Note: Currently alpha is stored but RRF doesn't use it directly
451: 
452:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
453:     async def test_hybrid_search_combines_results(self, default_user):
454:         """Test that hybrid search combines results from both dense and sparse search."""
455:         # Arrange
456:         query_text = "test query"
457:         query_embedding = torch.tensor([0.1, 0.2, 0.3])
458: 
459:         # Create overlapping results
460:         mock_entry_1 = create_mock_entry(1, "Entry from both")
461:         mock_entry_2 = create_mock_entry(2, "Entry from both")
462:         mock_entry_3 = create_mock_entry(3, "Dense only")
463:         mock_entry_4 = create_mock_entry(4, "Sparse only")
464: 
465:         dense_entries = [mock_entry_1, mock_entry_2, mock_entry_3]
466:         sparse_entries = [mock_entry_1, mock_entry_2, mock_entry_4]
467: 
468:         with patch("khoj.search_type.text_search.dense_search") as mock_dense, \
469:              patch("khoj.search_type.text_search.sparse_search") as mock_sparse:
470:             mock_dense.return_value = dense_entries
471:             mock_sparse.return_value = sparse_entries
472: 
473:             # Act
474:             results = await text_search.hybrid_search(
475:                 query_text=query_text,
476:                 query_embedding=query_embedding,
477:                 user=default_user,
478:                 k=10,
479:                 alpha=0.6,
480:             )
481: 
482:             # Assert
483:             result_ids = {entry.id for entry in results}
484:             # Should have all unique entries from both sources
485:             assert 1 in result_ids  # Common
486:             assert 2 in result_ids  # Common
487:             assert 3 in result_ids  # Dense only
488:             assert 4 in result_ids  # Sparse only
489: 
490:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
491:     async def test_hybrid_search_prefers_entries_in_both_sources(self, default_user):
492:         """Test that entries appearing in both dense and sparse results rank higher."""
493:         # Arrange
494:         query_text = "test query"
495:         query_embedding = torch.tensor([0.1, 0.2, 0.3])
496: 
497:         # Entry 1: Ranked high in both (should get highest RRF score)
498:         # Entry 2: Ranked high only in dense
499:         # Entry 3: Ranked high only in sparse
500:         mock_entry_1 = create_mock_entry(1, "Both sources")
501:         mock_entry_2 = create_mock_entry(2, "Dense only")
502:         mock_entry_3 = create_mock_entry(3, "Sparse only")
503: 
504:         dense_entries = [mock_entry_1, mock_entry_2]  # Entry 1 at rank 0
505:         sparse_entries = [mock_entry_1, mock_entry_3]  # Entry 1 at rank 0
506: 
507:         with patch("khoj.search_type.text_search.dense_search") as mock_dense, \
508:              patch("khoj.search_type.text_search.sparse_search") as mock_sparse:
509:             mock_dense.return_value = dense_entries
510:             mock_sparse.return_value = sparse_entries
511: 
512:             # Act
513:             results = await text_search.hybrid_search(
514:                 query_text=query_text,
515:                 query_embedding=query_embedding,
516:                 user=default_user,
517:                 k=10,
518:                 alpha=0.6,
519:             )
520: 
521:             # Assert
522:             # Entry 1 should be first as it appears in both lists
523:             assert results[0].id == 1
524: 
525:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
526:     async def test_hybrid_search_empty_dense_results(self, default_user):
527:         """Test hybrid search handles empty dense results gracefully."""
528:         # Arrange
529:         query_text = "test query"
530:         query_embedding = torch.tensor([0.1, 0.2, 0.3])
531: 
532:         mock_entry = create_mock_entry(1, "Sparse only entry")
533:         sparse_entries = [mock_entry]
534: 
535:         with patch("khoj.search_type.text_search.dense_search") as mock_dense, \
536:              patch("khoj.search_type.text_search.sparse_search") as mock_sparse:
537:             mock_dense.return_value = []
538:             mock_sparse.return_value = sparse_entries
539: 
540:             # Act
541:             results = await text_search.hybrid_search(
542:                 query_text=query_text,
543:                 query_embedding=query_embedding,
544:                 user=default_user,
545:                 k=10,
546:                 alpha=0.6,
547:             )
548: 
549:             # Assert
550:             assert len(results) == 1
551:             assert results[0].id == 1
552: 
553:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
554:     async def test_hybrid_search_empty_sparse_results(self, default_user):
555:         """Test hybrid search handles empty sparse results gracefully."""
556:         # Arrange
557:         query_text = "test query"
558:         query_embedding = torch.tensor([0.1, 0.2, 0.3])
559: 
560:         mock_entry = create_mock_entry(1, "Dense only entry")
561:         dense_entries = [mock_entry]
562: 
563:         with patch("khoj.search_type.text_search.dense_search") as mock_dense, \
564:              patch("khoj.search_type.text_search.sparse_search") as mock_sparse:
565:             mock_dense.return_value = dense_entries
566:             mock_sparse.return_value = []
567: 
568:             # Act
569:             results = await text_search.hybrid_search(

719:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
720:     async def test_hybrid_search_runs_concurrently(self, default_user):
721:         """Test that dense and sparse searches run concurrently (asyncio.gather)."""
722:         # Arrange
723:         query_text = "test query"
724:         query_embedding = torch.tensor([0.1, 0.2, 0.3])
725: 
726:         call_order = []
727: 
728:         async def tracked_dense_search(*args, **kwargs):
729:             call_order.append("dense")
730:             return [create_mock_entry(1)]
731: 
732:         async def tracked_sparse_search(*args, **kwargs):
733:             call_order.append("sparse")
734:             return [create_mock_entry(2)]
735: 
736:         with patch("khoj.search_type.text_search.dense_search", side_effect=tracked_dense_search), \
737:              patch("khoj.search_type.text_search.sparse_search", side_effect=tracked_sparse_search):
738:             # Act
739:             results = await text_search.hybrid_search(
740:                 query_text=query_text,
741:                 query_embedding=query_embedding,
742:                 user=default_user,
743:                 k=10,
744:                 alpha=0.6,
745:             )
746: 
747:             # Assert - if running concurrently, both should start before either completes
748:             # The order in call_order depends on which one schedules first
749:             assert "dense" in call_order
750:             assert "sparse" in call_order

894:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
895:     async def test_hybrid_search_with_many_overlapping_results(self, default_user):
896:         """Test hybrid search with many overlapping results."""
897:         # Arrange
898:         query_text = "test query"
899:         query_embedding = torch.tensor([0.1, 0.2, 0.3])
900: 
901:         # Create 20 entries that appear in both lists
902:         common_entries = [create_mock_entry(i, f"Common entry {i}") for i in range(1, 21)]
903:         # Add some unique entries
904:         dense_only = [create_mock_entry(i + 100, f"Dense only {i}") for i in range(1, 6)]
905:         sparse_only = [create_mock_entry(i + 200, f"Sparse only {i}") for i in range(1, 6)]
906: 
907:         with patch("khoj.search_type.text_search.dense_search") as mock_dense, \
908:              patch("khoj.search_type.text_search.sparse_search") as mock_sparse:
909:             mock_dense.return_value = common_entries + dense_only
910:             mock_sparse.return_value = common_entries + sparse_only
911: 
912:             # Act
913:             results = await text_search.hybrid_search(
914:                 query_text=query_text,
915:                 query_embedding=query_embedding,
916:                 user=default_user,
917:                 k=10,
918:                 alpha=0.6,
919:             )
920: 
921:             # Assert
922:             assert len(results) == 10
923:             # Common entries should rank higher (appear first)
924:             result_ids = {r.id for r in results}
925:             # All results should be unique
926:             assert len(result_ids) == 10
